sanskrit: सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च। मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम्॥
transliteration: sarva-dvārāṇi saṃyamya mano hṛdi nirudhya ca | mūrdhny ādhāyātmanaḥ prāṇam āsthito yoga-dhāraṇām ||
literal_meaning: Having restrained all the gates (senses), having confined the mind in the heart, and having placed the life-breath at the crown of the head, one is established in yogic concentration.
contextual_meaning: A protocol for deliberate sensory withdrawal and attentional redirection that culminates in a self-transcendent state; the verse maps a micro-to-macro arc from closing sense portals → heart-centered mindfulness → cortical apex awareness → unified absorption.
psychological_state: High-absorption trance marked by down-regulated default-mode network, up-regulated interoceptive focus, and a sense of vertical integration from heart to crown.
trigger_scenario: A knowledge-worker turns off all notifications, sits upright, breathes diaphragmatically, visualizes attention sinking into the chest, then rising to the top of the head—entering a 10-minute flow block before coding.
anchor_text: Shut the gates, sink into the heart, rise to the crown, and rest in oneness.
